metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Inline the nine E6 defect patterns directly in the plan (or in the AC) so the executor can enumerate them without consulting an external document.
findings.finding: The nine E6 defect patterns that must be enumerated in the rubric are not listed in the plan itself — the plan says 'the report lists them' and delegates enumeration to an external document (`~/dso_rebar_plan_review_gap_report.md`) that an executor may not have access to. This makes the E6 What bullet a placeholder deferring a real content decision to the executor.
findings.location: What / E6 bullet: 'ENUMERATE all nine patterns (the report lists them)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a numbered list of the nine E6 verify-command defect patterns directly under the E6 What bullet, e.g.: (1) word-boundary grep miss, (2) unquoted-key grep, (3) jq missing -e flag, (4) cardinality not asserted, (5) structured-artifact assertion replaced by prose check, (6) prerequisite ordering inversion, (7) … (fill remaining three from the report). This makes the plan self-contained and AC1 verifiable.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm and enumerate the exact nine E6 defect patterns in the plan (or correct the count to match the current rubric's six checks plus any net-new patterns from the gap report), so the AC is objectively verifiable against a codebase-grounded list.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'nine E6 patterns (the report lists them)' but the current plan_review_E6.md rubric has exactly six named checks (a)–(f). No codebase artifact corroborates a nine-pattern taxonomy; the count is unverifiable from the plan text and contradicts the current rubric structure.
findings.location: What section: 'E6: verify-command defect taxonomy -- ENUMERATE all nine patterns'
findings.suggested_fix: Read the DSO gap report and list all nine patterns inline in the plan's What section, OR correct the count to match the actual number of patterns being added (e.g. 'add three new patterns to the existing six checks: ...').

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Inline the nine E6 defect-pattern names directly in the plan (or the AC), so the executor can enumerate them without consulting an out-of-repo document.
findings.finding: The nine E6 defect-pattern enum values are referenced but never defined in the plan. The plan says 'ENUMERATE all nine patterns (the report lists them)' and points to an external file (`~/dso_rebar_plan_review_gap_report.md`) that is not in the repo. The current `plan_review_E6.md` has exactly six checks (a)–(f). An executor writing the E6 rubric enrichment must produce a nine-item closed vocabulary with no in-plan source for the three new patterns.
findings.location: ## What / E6 bullet: 'ENUMERATE all nine patterns (the report lists them)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a numbered list under the E6 What bullet enumerating the nine patterns, e.g.: (1) word-boundary/quoted-key grep miss, (2) jq missing -e flag, (3) cardinality not asserted, (4) structured-artifact assertion replaced by prose check, (5) prerequisite ordering inversion, (6)–(9) <fill from gap report>. This makes AC1 self-contained and verifiable.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name and briefly describe the three T3 empiricism axes directly in the plan so the executor can embed them in plan_review_T3.md.
findings.finding: The plan references 'three empiricism axes' as a T3 detection content addition but never names or defines the three axes. An executor writing the T3 rubric enrichment must embed these axes in the prompt but has no in-plan source for what they are.
findings.location: ## What / T3 bullet: 'three empiricism axes + a four-class evidence scale'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a one-sentence definition for each of bypass-annotation, scope-narrowing, and partial-without-remainder directly in the plan's G3 What bullet.
findings.finding: G3's new terms 'bypass-annotation', 'scope-narrowing', and 'partial-without-remainder' are declared as deliverables to DEFINE in the prompt, but no definitions are provided in the plan. These are decision-rule vocabulary that the LLM must apply when classifying coverage — without definitions, the G3 rubric enrichment cannot be written by an executor unfamiliar with the gap-report.
findings.location: ## What / G3 bullet: 'DEFINE bypass-annotation / scope-narrowing / partial-without-remainder'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define 'consumer-enumeration precursor' and 'recall->worklist' operationally in the plan: what does the worklist contain, how does it differ from the existing consumer-impact mode, and does it replace or augment one of the seven cross-child modes?
findings.finding: G4's new mode 'consumer-enumeration precursor (recall->worklist over the existing consumer-impact mode)' is a processing protocol change referenced without defining what the worklist is, how it differs from the existing 'consumer impact' check, or what recall→worklist means operationally. The existing plan_review_G4.md lists seven cross-child interaction modes; the plan proposes replacing/augmenting 'consumer impact' with a worklist idiom but provides no definition of the new protocol.
findings.location: ## What / G4 bullet: 'G4: consumer-enumeration precursor (recall->worklist over the existing consumer-impact mode)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the sidecar conflict: either (a) update the plan to NOT claim 'sidecar carries it' (since evidence[] is dropped by design), or (b) add a sidecar.py change to `_slim()` as an explicit deliverable so the T3 class IS persisted.
findings.finding: The plan instructs that the T3 four-class evidence annotation should be 'recorded in Pass-1 evidence[] only' and that 'sidecar carries it' — but `sidecar.py`'s `_slim()` function explicitly EXCLUDES `evidence` from sidecar payloads (it lists `evidence` as a 'deliberately excluded runtime-only carrier'). This is a contradicted structural placement: the plan's protocol and the existing sidecar contract are incompatible, with no plan change to `sidecar.py` proposed.
findings.location: ## What / T3 bullet: 'recorded in Pass-1 evidence[] only. Fallback rule… Confirm P1Finding.evidence is list[str] and sidecar carries it'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command or eval assertion for each of the G3, T5c, T11, E2, and G4 rubric changes (e.g. extend `test_plan_review_evals.py` with recall fixtures that would only pass if the new G3/T5c/T11/E2/G4 prompt content is present and correct), or state an explicit rationale for why AC4's existing pytest suite is sufficient to cover them.
findings.finding: The G3, T5c, T11, E2, and G4 rubric changes listed in the What section have no concrete proving command in any AC — they are subsumed under AC1's 'each named rubric file updated as above', where 'as above' itself defers to the external gap report for correctness criteria. No deterministic check distinguishes a correct G3/T5c/T11/E2/G4 update from an incorrect one.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria / AC1; What section last bullet (G3, T5c, T11, E2, G4)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a definition for each T3 evidence class (Verified/Partial/Unverified/Contradicted) directly in the plan — one sentence per class minimum — so the prompt author has an authoritative source.
findings.finding: The four T3 evidence-class values (Verified/Partial/Unverified/Contradicted) are named in the plan but never defined. The plan states 'each class DEFINED' yet supplies no definitions for what qualifies a finding's evidence as Verified vs Partial vs Contradicted. An LLM executing the T3 rubric enrichment must embed definitions in the prompt but has no authoritative source for them in the plan.
findings.location: ## What / T3 bullet: 'four-class evidence scale Verified/Partial/Unverified/Contradicted, each class DEFINED'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify whether 'sidecar carries it' is an intended new deliverable (requires modifying sidecar.py's _slim() to add evidence) or a documentation error (the T3 evidence lives only in the in-memory P1Finding, not the persisted sidecar), and update the AC accordingly.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'sidecar carries [T3 evidence]' (T3 change recorded in Pass-1 evidence[] only; 'sidecar carries it'), but sidecar.py's build_payload explicitly EXCLUDES the evidence field from the persisted sidecar payload, listing it as a 'runtime-only carrier' that is deliberately not stored.
findings.location: What section: T3 change — 'recorded in Pass-1 evidence[] only... Confirm P1Finding.evidence is list[str] and sidecar carries it'
findings.suggested_fix: If evidence persistence is NOT intended, change 'sidecar carries it' to 'P1Finding.evidence is list[str] (already confirmed in passes.py)'. If evidence persistence IS a new deliverable, add an explicit AC item to update sidecar.py's _slim() function.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether 'sidecar carries it' (T3 evidence[]) requires a sidecar.py/_slim() code change; if yes, extend the AC proof to cover sidecar.py (or explicitly scope it out of this ticket as a follow-on), so the 'prompt-only' claim and the AC proof are self-consistent.
findings.finding: The T3 sub-item asserts 'sidecar carries it' (evidence[]) as part of the Pass-1-only change, but sidecar.py's _slim() deliberately EXCLUDES evidence[] by design ('exclude runtime-only carriers (e.g. scenarios, evidence, _agentic)'). Making the sidecar carry evidence requires a code change to sidecar.py that is not a prompt-only change and is not guarded by the AC proof ('git diff --name-only touches no plan_review_verifier*.md and no passes.py Pass-2/4 code'). The AC proof covers only verifier and passes.py — sidecar.py is outside that guard — so the AC is satisfiable while the sidecar evidence carry silently slips through unverified, or the 'prompt-only' framing is wrong for this sub-item.
findings.location: What section — T3 sub-item; AC line 'T3 change is Pass-1-only. Proof: git diff --name-only touches no plan_review_verifier*.md and no passes.py Pass-2/4 code.'
findings.suggested_fix: Either: (a) remove 'sidecar carries it' from the T3 deliverable scope (evidence classes live in the prompt only, not persisted) and keep the plan truly prompt-only; or (b) acknowledge sidecar.py is in scope, add it to the AC proof guard ('git diff --name-only touches no plan_review_verifier*.md and no passes.py Pass-2/4 code AND sidecar._slim includes evidence[]').

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or extend AC2/AC4) that explicitly verifies `P1Finding.evidence` is typed `list[str]` in the sidecar schema — e.g. `python -c 'from rebar.llm.plan_review.models import P1Finding; assert P1Finding.__fields__["evidence"].outer_type_ is list'` or a dedicated unit test.
findings.finding: The T3 What section describes a non-prompt task — 'Confirm P1Finding.evidence is list[str] and sidecar carries it' — that has no covering AC verifying it was completed, leaving described work without a measurable acceptance criterion.
findings.location: What section, T3 bullet: 'Confirm P1Finding.evidence is list[str] and sidecar carries it'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC2: 'Proof also includes: `python -c "from rebar.llm.plan_review.models import P1Finding; import typing; assert typing.get_origin(P1Finding.__fields__[\"evidence\"].outer_type_) is list"` exits 0.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one end-state-observable proof (e.g., a quoted key phrase to grep in the updated file, or a named eval test) for each of G3, T5c, T11, E2, and G4 in the Acceptance Criteria.
findings.finding: Five of the seven named rubric changes (G3, T5c, T11, E2, G4) have no end-state-observable proof in the ACs — only E6 (nine patterns) and T3 (four-class scale) are explicitly verified by the AC list. If G3/T5c/T11/E2/G4 changes are delivered incompletely or not at all, no AC catches it.
findings.location: ## Acceptance Criteria (all four bullet lines) vs. ## What (G3, T5c, T11, E2, G4 bullets)
findings.suggested_fix: For each of G3/T5c/T11/E2/G4, add an AC sub-bullet such as: 'G3: `grep -q "bypass-annotation" reviewers/plan_review_g3.md` exits 0' — one anchor phrase per criterion is sufficient to make the deliverable falsifiable.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the potential contradiction: either (a) assert in the plan that P1Finding.evidence is already list[str] (no code change needed, confirm-only), or (b) explicitly carve out the model-schema fix from the 'pure prompt work' characterization and adjust AC2's proof accordingly.
findings.finding: The 'Confirm P1Finding.evidence is list[str]' task in the T3 What section may require a code change (schema/model edit), which would contradict both the 'pure prompt work' characterization in Why and AC2's proof constraint ('git diff touches no plan_review_verifier*.md and no passes.py Pass-2/4 code') — creating a cross-section contradiction if code must change.
findings.location: Why section ('pure prompt work') vs. What/T3 bullet ('Confirm P1Finding.evidence is list[str] and sidecar carries it') vs. AC2 proof constraint
findings.suggested_fix: Add a parenthetical to the T3 bullet: '(already list[str] — confirm only, no code change)' if that is the case; otherwise move the schema fix to a separate AC outside AC2's git-diff scope.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether 'Confirm P1Finding.evidence is list[str]' requires a code change or is a read-only verification, and if the former, update the title/framing to reflect that the ticket is not purely prompt-only.
findings.finding: The plan's headline and framing say 'prompt-only' but T3's scope explicitly includes a structural data-contract check ('Confirm P1Finding.evidence is list[str] and sidecar carries it'), which is a code/schema verification step, not a prompt edit. This mismatch could mislead the implementer about what files to touch and what tests to run.
findings.suggested_fix: If the confirmation is read-only (the type is already correct), say so explicitly: 'Verify (no change needed) that P1Finding.evidence is already list[str].' If a code fix might be needed, change the title to 'prompt and schema' and add an AC for the struct check.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the `file_impact` section to the plan (or inline the specific file paths per criterion), resolving the glob `reviewers/plan_review_*.md` to the exact filenames for each of E6, T3, G3, T5c, T11, E2, and G4.
findings.finding: The plan references a `file_impact` section inline ('Concrete code targets are named inline and in this ticket's file_impact') but no `file_impact` section appears in the plan. The specific rubric file paths (e.g. `reviewers/plan_review_*.md`) are named only generically; an executor cannot determine which exact files to edit without the missing section.
findings.location: Provenance note: 'Concrete code targets are named inline and in this ticket's file_impact'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out step to the plan — e.g. 'if post-merge eval regression > N%, revert via `git revert <sha>`' or 'run full prompt eval suite (`pytest tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py -k full`) before merging' — so the reversibility mechanism is named, not just implied by git history.
findings.finding: The plan modifies the detection behavior of multiple existing rubric criteria (E6, T3, G3, T5c, T11, E2, G4) — criteria that downstream review runs and eval baselines already rely on — without stating an explicit rollback or back-out path if the sharpened prompts cause unexpected regressions beyond what AC4 evals cover.
findings.location: What section (all bullet items); AC3, AC4

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether the E6 eval AC refers to pre-existing cases (already passing, no action needed) or whether WS5 requires adding NEW eval cases for the nine-pattern taxonomy; if the latter, name the specific new case IDs this plan will add.
findings.finding: The plan's AC states 'Affected recall evals updated. Proof: pytest tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py green (incl. R-E6-cutover-no-live-ac)' — but R-E6-cutover-no-live-ac already exists in the shipped eval YAML (plan-review-finder.eval.yaml line 78) and is already asserted in test_plan_review_evals.py line 111. The plan frames this as a deliverable to create, but the state it describes is already satisfied.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, fourth bullet: 'Affected recall evals updated. Proof: pytest tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py green (incl. R-E6-cutover-no-live-ac)'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
